title: Color Sensing by Reflectance — TCS3200 Frequency-Mode Operation
content: The TCS3200 colour sensor contains an 8×8 array of photodiodes with selectable R/G/B/clear filters. Each filter set is selected via S2/S3 pins; the output is a square wave whose frequency is proportional to the light intensity of that colour component. Scaling via S0/S1 pins: 100 %, 20 %, or 2 % of full-scale output frequency.
extended_content: Measurement principle: select filter (e.g., R: S2=LOW, S3=LOW), measure output pulse frequency for 20–100 ms window using MCU timer/interrupt. Repeat for G, B, and Clear. RGB values normalised by clear channel to compensate ambient light variation. White-balance calibration: measure white reference and black reference, then apply linear scaling per channel: R_norm = (R_raw − R_black) / (R_white − R_black) × 255. Full-scale frequency at 100 % scaling: ~600 kHz under 1000 lux white light. Practical reading distance: 10–15 mm with diffuse white LED illumination. TCS34725 (I²C) is the newer alternative with RGB+clear 16-bit ADC and interrupt support; preferred for new designs. Common application: object colour classification on a conveyor (RED/GREEN/BLUE sorting bins).
source: ams TCS3200 Programmable Color Light-to-Frequency Converter Data Sheet; TCS34725 Application Note
